title: refund — compliance evidence
description: Refund module is financial-record-critical (every refund is a reversal that impacts recognised revenue — SOX + tax audit relevant) + PII-adjacent (customer_id + initiated_by_user_id + approved_by_user_id captured on every refund) + advertising-adjacent (feeds negative-value marketing events for ROAS adjustment) + consumer-protection-adjacent (regulator-mandated refund SLAs vary 14-30 days by jurisdiction). GDPR (Art. 5, 6, 17), CAN-SPAM Act (refund notifications), US tax law (7y financial record), SOX 404 (internal controls + segregation of duties), SOC 2 CC6.3, PCI-DSS (token-based reversal, no card data).

controls.evidence: Every refund records initiator + approver (when applicable) + timestamp + reason — full auditability. Terms of the reversal frozen at succeeded state (amount + currency + reason) — no post-hoc mutation. Customer notified at every state transition (RefundRequested / RefundApproved / RefundProcessed / RefundSucceeded / RefundFailed / RefundRejected).

Refund captures only what the reversal computation needs: reference to parent payment + invoice + reason + amount. No incidental PII beyond initiated_by_user_id + approved_by_user_id which are FKs to the user module.
7-year retention window justified by tax + SOX financial-record obligations. Enterprise 10-year extension for regulated tenants. Automated purge via PurgeExpiredRefundDataJob.
Refund processing is contractual — the tenant/customer agreement + consumer-protection law both mandate refund handling. Terms of refund + reasons + approval workflow constitute the contract.

UserErased (as any actor on refunds) triggers RedactCustomerFromRefundsOnUserErased — 90 days post-erasure, redact user_id references to a hashed placeholder. Row survives for the 7-year financial audit trail (financial-obligation carve-out per Art. 17 §3(b)).

No fully-automated decisions with legal/significant effect. Approval is human-decided (approver clicks approve/reject); the requires_approval computation is rule-based (thresholds + reasons) but auto-approval only applies to explicit reasons (duplicate_charge is system-initiated by finance ops). Refusal responses always include an actionable reason code + support-contact CTA.

Refund is IMMUTABLE post-succeeded. Segregation of duties enforced (approver ≠ initiator). Customer-scope middleware blocks cross-customer refund lookups. Confidential financial fields (amount_cents, provider_fee_reversal_cents) require refunds.view-financials permission.

Every notification is transactional (state transitions, financial reversals) — cannot-opt-out per US CAN-SPAM 4(a)(3)(a) (required for the refund-flow disclosure). No promotional refund content exists — refund is a contractual event, not marketing.
Every refund email footer includes the tenant's registered business address (populated from tenant.legal_address_json).

Notification email subjects are fixed shape: 'Your refund request has been received' / 'Your refund has been approved' / 'Your refund is processing' / 'Your refund is complete' / 'Your refund could not be processed' — no clickbait / misleading language.

controls.principle: traceable audit trail of every revenue reversal
controls.evidence: Every succeeded refund carries: amount_cents, currency, reason, refund_type + a linked credit_note_id (reversal invoice via invoice module) + a linked transaction_id (offsetting double-entry ledger via transaction module). Cross-referenced with parent payment + invoice for full revenue-recognition audit. Retained 7 years.

controls.principle: record of tax reversal per jurisdiction
controls.evidence: The parent invoice carries jurisdiction via finance::tax::TaxCalculation. The credit note issued on succeeded refund carries the tax reversal per line. Reduces output VAT / sales tax for the reporting period.

controls.criterion: SOX 404 — internal controls over financial reporting
controls.principle: segregation of duties + audit trail on reversal authorization
controls.evidence: Refund.create requires finance/admin/owner role OR customer for own refunds. Refund.approve requires refunds.approve permission AND approver ≠ initiator (REFUSED via RefundPolicy::approve). Every write + reversal is Auditable (7-year retention). Terms are IMMUTABLE post-succeeded (REFUND_IMMUTABLE). Force-fail restricted to admin+owner only.

Refund CRUD requires finance/admin/owner. Approve/reject requires refunds.approve + approver ≠ initiator. Force-fail requires admin/owner only. Confidential financial fields (amount_cents, provider_fee_reversal_cents) require refunds.view-financials permission. Platform-plane cross-tenant read requires super_admin/ops/support role.
All routes are TLS-only + require auth:sanctum (tenant) or auth:platform_admin (platform). refund.enforce_customer_scope middleware blocks cross-customer refund access.

academorix.refund.atomicity.failures_total = 0 target. academorix.refund.finalization_failures_total = 0 target. Every RefundSucceeded fires the finalization chain + records metrics; failures dispatch RefundProviderError + alert.

Sum-invariant across refunds prevents over-refunding. Immutable-after-succeeded prevents accidental mutation. Approval workflow prevents unauthorized reversals. Reconciler job catches provider-side state drift. Force-fail escape hatch for stuck refunds.
Refund + RefundLine rows are Auditable (7-year retention). Every state transition + every field mutation + every reversal audit-logged. Audit-log entries survive cascade deletes.

Two-guard permission surface (sanctum + platform_admin). Confidential financial fields restricted to finance+admin+support+owner. Financial reversal (approve/force-fail) restricted per role. Bulk-processing restricted to Enterprise + admin+owner.
The refund-status-chip + reason-picker widgets use HeroUI's design tokens which meet AA contrast by default. Status colours never rely on colour alone — each carries a text label.
Refund request wizard + approve/reject modal + refund-line editor are fully keyboard-navigable per HeroUI ComboBox + Modal + Form semantics.

Every observer refusal returns a specific error code (REFUND_CAPTURE_NOT_ENTITLED, REFUND_AMOUNT_EXCEEDS_REFUNDABLE, REFUND_SELF_APPROVAL_REFUSED, etc.) + user-facing message. Refund refusal responses include actionable reason + support-contact CTA when appropriate.

Approval SLA metric (academorix.refund.approvals_stale_total) fires P2 signals when refunds sit > 24h in awaiting_approval. Provider-side dispatch SLA (5-10 days for card refunds) is provider-communicated + surfaced to customer via RefundProcessedNotification. Force-fail escape hatch for stuck refunds (> 240 poll attempts = 10 days at hourly cadence).
14-30 day refund windows for specific purchase types. Refund SLA monitored via metrics. Records retained 3 years minimum (7y default exceeds).

14-day cooling-off period. Refund requests via POST /refunds/mine within 14 days of purchase auto-approve for eligible purchase types (configurable via refund.reason.auto_approve when tenant enables customer-driven cooling-off).
Statutory rights refund windows retained 6 years minimum (7y default exceeds).

controls.principle: audit trail retained 1y online + 3y total minimum
controls.evidence: Refund records retained 7y (10y Enterprise) — exceeds PCI-DSS minimum. Every reversal records the parent payment's provider_reference_id (token, never PAN) + provider_fee_reversal_cents.

Refund uses provider token from parent payment (payment.provider_reference_id). Refund NEVER receives / stores / logs card PAN / CVV / magnetic stripe / PIN — the refund provider driver dispatches the reversal using the token.
